text: Lots of volatility and large volume again in the UK morning session. What’s becoming increasingly clear is that there’s rotation happening - a change in who’s holding the stock - and I view that as a strong positive. Yesterday’s wick lower looked very similar to what we saw in the U.S. a couple of weeks ago, when there was clear capitulation in $TSWCF from one (or more) sellers, followed by an aggressive bounce. Today’s price action and volume reinforce the idea: there are still investors looking to exit, but also clear demand from buyers stepping in and that’s encouraging. It’s a bit surprising to see some selling here, especially given the skewed risk/reward setup: we’re trading around 1.3x mNAV, and sentiment across the sector is already washed out. But for investors with a longer time horizon, there’s real value here. Expect continued volatility; 30-day realised vol is currently 150, meaning the stock’s averaging ~9.5% daily moves but as long as volume stays elevated, we’re in a strong position to continue raising cash and buying Bitcoin where it is accretive and moving steadily toward the 3,000 BTC target.

text: Bullish Research Note from Tennyson Securities - Maintain BUY rating $SWC the UK’s flagship Bitcoin treasury company, has announced a £2.5,m Bitcoin purchase. This comes shortly after a £2.6 million equity raise via its ATM facility. Increases SWC’s Bitcoin holdings from 2,440 to 2,470. SWC is now the 25th largest public company globally in terms of Bitcoin held on its balance sheet. Sats per share increased 1.2%, from 907 to 918. The company’s 30-day Bitcoin yield stands at 16%, or 0.5% per day. Since its IPO in April (140 days ago), SWC has raised £204m in external capital. 99% of that capital has been used for Bitcoin purchases. SWC’s Bitcoin treasury is currently valued at £203m The company has generated an approximate gain of £1.1m on its Bitcoin holdings since going public. Signs are emerging that mNAV compression (which affected August sentiment) is starting to bottom out. Today’s update highlights that SWC’s ATM facility remains a major competitive advantage. SWC is still the only UK-listed company with an active ATM facility. The ability to raise equity at market and fund accretive Bitcoin purchases helps maintain Bitcoin yield in tougher markets. With an mNAV of 2.1x and a P/BYD of 0.4x, the stock remains attractively valued. The BUY recommendation is maintained.

text: Very encouraging price action so far, especially following yesterday’s +30% move. It would have been discouraging to give back a significant portion, but we’re currently down just ~4%, which is very constructive considering the strong momentum up from the previous four sessions. Importantly, today’s volume has remained solid, helping to establish a healthy base around these levels. This consolidation is critical - while volatility can be exciting, measured periods of stability often provide a more sustainable setup for future moves. In fact, this kind of steadiness is particularly valuable as it creates a more favorable environment for potential capital markets activity, unlike sharp, parabolic spikes that can be harder to manage and sustain.

text: The Power of Momentum. Today’s +30% move in $SWC is just the beginning and I think we could see people chasing the momentum. Starting at 172.5p, if we see just five consecutive 30% moves, here’s what happens; •172.5p → 224p •224p → 291p •291p → 380p •380p → 492p •492p → 640p That’s nearly a 3.7× move in only 5 days, taking us right back to all-time highs. 🔥 That’s the raw power of compounding momentum. One thing I’ve learned in crypto is anything can happen and if we get some positive sentiment into the sector (could be after the metaplanet international pricing which sees the stock bounce) we could be in for some big moves.

engagement.note: These figures were correct as of May 14, 2026, 9:00 AM London time

text: What a session in London, closing +30% (172.5p) with the US still open and scope to keep up the momentum. It’s amazing how much can change in just a week - this time last week the share price felt like it was going to trade sub 100p and it's a reminder of why we have to keep believing in the process and trusting the fundamentals. We’re not completely out of the woods yet, but there’s a lot of light breaking through on the other side of the tunnel. I continue to believe that a lot of the large sellers who had been leaning on the price have been cleared out. I’m proud that during the toughest moments of the last few weeks (especially last when we were at the lows), everyone dug in, put their best foot forward, and showed why we’ve always believed the fundamentals would ultimately shine through and that we’d emerge stronger. I’ve got a great feeling about the rest of the year and what lies beyond.

text: On Friday, I mentioned that I wanted to see sustained positive price action supported by increasing volume to give this rebound more credibility. That’s exactly what we’re seeing this morning, with healthy volume going through the London line. This also reinforces the view that last Tuesday’s (9/2) sharp move lower, marked by a significant wick down during the US session on outsized volume, was likely the “capitulation event,” clearing out one or more persistent sellers who had been weighing on the price for some time. Of course, I want to stay grounded in my expectations - $SWC’s price remains closely tied to Bitcoin, broader market sentiment, flows, and news. That said, things are looking constructive so far this week. It feels like we’re finally moving into clearer skies as we aim to reclaim a level above 2x mNAV (currently sitting around ~1.9x).

text: Summary of the Smarter Web call between @asjwebley and @Croesus_BTC TLDR: A very constructive and positive call. SWC continues to build momentum through disciplined capital strategy, strong community support, and sustained Bitcoin yield, even in a tough market. With institutional interest growing and key foundations in place, the company is well-positioned for the next phase of growth. * These are my own interpretations, with some personal additions included. Market context and price action • The team has spent a lot of time understanding and communicating what they feel are the drivers of the recent sector compression - $SWC 's recent volatility has largely been driven by sector sentiment as fundamentals have continued to improved. • Despite persistent market volatility, $SWC continues to build and focus across both the operating and treasury businesses. • Raised £35m last month across various channels; placing, TSC, ATM, and direct subscription • Raising over £1m/day, even in challenging market conditions (pretty"okay" for now). Capital raising strategy and execution • The team believes the capital raising strategies currently in place, especially the ATM, are best suited for this stage of SWC's growth. • ATM remains the most critical part of the capital toolkit: It acts as a base layer to stack Bitcoin steadily. Broker data and internal testing confirm minimal market impact up to 20% of daily volume. • Andrew emphasised that while other treasury strategies (e.g. pref shares, potential derivative strategies) have been discussed, many are more appropriate for later stages of scale (e.g., pref shares would require SWC to be 10x larger). • The team continues to explore new tools responsibly, but is confident that the current strategy is optimal for where they are today. • Andrew emphasised the importance of clear communication and transparency around SWC's strategy and decisions. • Andrew also noted that capital raising is central to a BTCTC’s credibility, and SWC has successfully executed multiple rounds, building momentum and trust with brokers and investors. Putting shareholders first • Over the past month, the company has had interest from capital pools in the £100–250m range, though this came with strings (e.g. significant discounts). • As always, Andrew reiterated the importance of only raising capital on good terms, and avoiding heavily discounted deals unless absolutely necessary. SWC’s goal remains to grow BTC per share, but not at any cost. Institutional capital engagement • The team is working across multiple capital channels: Andrew is focused on UK institutions, and there is optimism that some are nearing readiness to commit. • In the U.S., they are engaging with three banks, aiming to form a possible relationship with at least one. • Engagement with smaller global funds and family offices continues, and all leads from Hong Kong are being followed up. • Meetings are planned around Token 2049 in Singapore. • Institutional capital typically prefers to enter “just off the bottom”, looking for confirmation of returned momentum (like we've seen over the past 3 sessions). • Many are comfortable paying 10–20% higher if it means avoiding false bottoms. • The team believes this dynamic is reflected in recent price action. • Portfolio managers are now back from summer, which, combined with improved sentiment, should lead to increased engagement. • Jesse and Andrew both emphasized that institutions take time, but SWC is positioned well to benefit from a rotation back to quality. Strategic positioning in the market • Jesse reiterated that the company’s core focus is on generating Bitcoin yield and despite tough conditions, $SWC has delivered 28% yield over the past 5 weeks. • Believe the recent market contraction is flushing out opportunistic BTCTCs that failed to gain traction after the initial wave which saturated the sector. • Many of these companies couldn’t build the operational flywheel or community support to survive. • In contrast, $SWC has executed well, reaching the level of maturity and scale necessary to weather this storm - continuing to generate yield and access capital, albeit at a more measured pace during the summer months where investors are away. • As a result, $SWC is now significantly de-risked compared to earlier-stage peers and is poised to benefit from the next phase of mNAV expansion, expected this fall. Convert product • The convertible product continues to generate interest. • Some initial targets have shifted toward equity discussions instead, surprising the team somewhat. • Andrew believes the convert's structure is excellent, offering large holders upside participation with downside protection. • Current hesitation may stem from it appearing “too good to be true” to some but confidence remains that more investors will come onboard as understanding increases. Treasury tools and future options • There’s always ongoing internal discussion around expanding the treasury playbook. • If mNAV were to drop below 1x, the team might explore using a small part of the treasury to sell Bitcoin put options, generating additional cash yield. • Andrew believes this idea is premature for now, but acknowledged it’s their responsibility to continuously explore new strategies. Community & liquidity strength •Andrew highlighted two additional strategic strengths: • Third-largest community in the space (4,200 members) and strong stock liquidity, which enhances institutional appeal. • Combined, these give SWC a unique position of credibility and momentum as a BTCTC. Thank you for reading and let's keep pushing forward together!

text: What's going on with $SWC ? Worth reposting the below from last week but today's sell-off has far more to do with broader market sentiment and flows than with anything specific to $SWC , where news flow and fundamentals remain positive. At the macro level, markets are in a clear “risk-off / flight to safety” mode. The sell off in government bonds has pushed yields higher, especially at the long end of the curve, which is rippling through into equities. Gold trading above $3,500 underscores this dynamic. Remember, BTCTCs are a “risk-on” sector: they tend to outperform when sentiment is strong and underperform when risk appetite fades. That’s exactly what we’re seeing, with US tech names also trading lower. On the flow side, it feels like one (or several) sellers continue to press bids to force liquidity. No one knows the exact profile, whether it’s speculative money cutting losses after entering higher, an IPO holder reducing, or something else entirely and speculating isn’t useful. What matters is that the price action is disconnected from fundamentals. RSI shows we are at deeply oversold levels, which suggests we may be nearing the end of this capitulation phase. Once selling pressure exhausts, the stock should have room to reset. Personally, I haven’t sold a single share of $SWC and remain confident in both the outlook and execution. If I had more cash, I would be buying here (nfa). x.com/the_desert_ape/status/…

text: Bullish note from Tennyson Securities - maintain BUY The Smarter Web Company - Appoints CFO & Adviser Ahead Of Likely LSE Uplisting The UK’s flagship Bitcoin Treasury Company, The Smarter Web Company has announced the appointment of both a Chief Financial Officer and a new Corporate Adviser ahead of a likely uplisting to the LSE main market in the coming months. New CFO, Albert Soleiman was previously CFO of CMC Markets, a FTSE 250 financial services company, where he was responsible for “building out its investment platform and leading a strategic alignment of the business.” New Corporate Adviser, Strand Hanson , brings a wealth of experience advising UK companies, including as an LSE Sponsor, “navigating the regulatory framework of the Main Market”. $SWC shares continue to look attractive relative to its global peers, especially given its outstanding track record of delivering market-leading BTC yield, backed by a combination of institutional equity raises and its unique ATM facility. In the 131 days since its IPO in April, SWC has raised £202m of external capital, of which 99% has been deployed for Bitcoin purchases, enabling the company to increase its sats per share (SPS) from 2 to 914 over the same period. $SWC shares now trade on an mNAV multiple of just 1.6x at current levels, significantly below Capital B, Satsuma and H100 We maintain our BUY recommendation.
